title: Caiophora sylvestris
taxonomy: Caiophora sylvestris is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It belongs to the genus Caiophora, which is a group of flowering plants native to South America. The family Polemoniaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the phlox or gilia family. Caiophora sylvestris is typically found growing in mountainous regions and is known for its unique and attractive flowers. This species is further classified into various ranks within the plant kingdom, including kingdom Plantae, division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Ericales.

intro: Caiophora sylvestris, also known as the "Andean clover," is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae, native to the Andean regions of South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is used to alleviate symptoms such as pain, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Caiophora sylvestris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional Andean medicine, Caiophora sylvestris is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in the production of tinctures and topical creams.
